title: Hopea selanica
intro: Hopea selanica, also known as Borneo ironwood, is a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae, native to the island of Borneo. The health benefits of Hopea selanica include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been reported to exhibit potential in treating various conditions such as fever, headache, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Hopea selanica is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Hopea selanica is classified as a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae. It belongs to the genus Hopea, which consists of various species of trees found in Southeast Asia. The family Dipterocarpaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the dipterocarps, which are native to tropical Asia and Australia. These trees are known for their large size, distinctive seed pods, and valuable timber. Hopea selanica is a native species to Southeast Asia, specifically in Malaysia and Indonesia.
uses: Hopea selanica is said to have various medicinal uses in traditional medicine. It is reported to be used in the treatment of fever, cough, and digestive issues. The plant's leaves are believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate pain and reduce swelling. Additionally, it is also used to treat skin conditions and wounds due to its antibacterial and antifungal properties. However, its medicinal uses are not extensively scientifically studied and more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
